channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump to unveil 'Liberation Day' tariffs, raising trade tensions
item.description: The tariffs goals include reducing the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. and boosting American-made manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers take action after report shows Biden-era SBA failed to probe 2 million alleged COVID aid fraudsters
item.description: Small Business Committee chairs Joni Ernst and Roger Williams plan to launch an effort to help bring COVID fraudsters to justice by extending the statute of limitation a decade.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How couriers changed the playing field of the Texas Lottery
item.description: A Texas Tribune investigation reveals how the third-party services operate in the state and the impact of online sales on the lottery’s games.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP senators push ahead on Trump's tax cuts package, punting big decisions for later
item.description: Senate Republicans insist they are pushing ahead on President Donald Trump’s big bill of tax breaks and spending cuts this week.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic-backed Wisconsin state schools chief Jill Underly wins reelection over GOP-backed rival
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin voted Tuesday for Jill Underly to remain as the state's top education official during President Donald Trump’s second term, choosing the Democratic-backed incumbent over a Republican-supported critic.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER and TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With a nod to America's civil rights legacy, Sen. Cory Booker makes a mark of his own
item.description: Democratic Sen. Cory Booker broke the record for the longest floor speech in recorded Senate history when he bested Sen. Strom Thurmond's filibuster of the Civil Rights Act of 1957.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP retains two House seats in Florida, Democrats claim 'historic' improvement
item.description: Republicans won special elections in two Florida Congressional districts. The margins of victory in the heavily-Republican districts were significantly narrower than in November.
item.dc:creator: Greg Allen, MPR News

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: Susan Crawford, a Dane County judge who led legal fights to protect union power and abortion rights and to oppose voter ID, defeated Republican-backed Brad Schimel in a race that broke records for spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Liberal wins first major 2025 statewide battleground election in race turned into Trump-Musk referendum
item.description: The liberal candidate won in the first major statewide battleground election of 2025, protecting the progressive majority on the Wisconsin's supreme court, in a race that became a referendum on President Donald Trump and Elon Musk, and broke records as the most expense court election in the nat
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former DeSantis official defeats Dem for Matt Gaetz's House seat
item.description: A special election in Florida's 1st Congressional District between Republican Jimmy Patronis and Democrat Gay Valimont was held to determine the successor to Matt Gaetz's House seat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans seek to block the reappointment of UN official accused of antisemitism
item.description: The House Foreign Affairs Committee is pushing the United Nations to not reappoint Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese for another term over accusations of antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration halts dozens of research grants at Princeton University
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has halted dozens of research grants at Princeton University, the latest Ivy League school to see its federal money threatened in a pressure campaign targeting the nation’s top universities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mass layoffs are underway at the nation's public health agencies
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department received notices Tuesday that their jobs were being eliminated, part of a sweeping overhaul designed to vastly shrink the agencies responsible for protecting and promoting Americans’ health.
item.dc:creator: CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zeldin to visit border where 'disgusting Mexican sewage' is harming US environment
item.description: Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Administrator Lee Zeldin said he plans to visit the border to assess toxic waste spilling over from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speaker Johnson suffers a defeat in his push to block parents in the U.S. House from proxy voting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Speaker Mike Johnson endured a decisive defeat Tuesday after he staged an unusually aggressive effort to squash a proposal for new parents in Congress to able to vote by proxy, rather than in person, as they care for newborns.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to deliver 'Liberation Day' announcement from Rose Garden flanked by Cabinet members
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to unveil his Liberation Day tariff plan in the White House's Rose Garden Wednesday in an effort to end decades-long reliance on goods made overseas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge blocks firing of intelligence agency employees who worked on DEI programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge in Virginia blocked the Trump administration's move to fire more than a dozen intelligence agency employees who worked on diversity, equity and inclusion programs. U.S. District Judge Anthony J.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House warns of Trump veto if Tim Kaine 'stunt' vote to cancel tariffs passes Senate
item.description: The White House is preparing to potentially veto a resolution to undo tariffs on Canada, which will have a privileged vote tentatively on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Emerging GOP leader backing Trump’s use of Alien Enemies Act at Supreme Court
item.description: Congressman Brandon Gill is teaming with America First Legal to file an amicus brief to the Supreme Court backing President Donald Trump’s use of the Alien Enemies Act to deport violent gang members.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SCOOP: Trump ally Donalds showcases campaign cash surge since announcing Florida governor run
item.description: Republican Rep. Byron Donalds of Florida, a top House ally of President Trump, hauls in $12 million in fundraising in the first 35 days since announcing his 2026 run for governor, his team says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Senate vote to reverse Trump's tariffs on Canada tests Republican support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With President Donald Trump's so-called “Liberation Day” of tariff implementation fast approaching, Senate Democrats are putting Republican support for some of those plans to the test by forcing a vote to nullify the emergency declaration that underpins the tariffs on Canada.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge in long-running lawsuit declines to block the use of Georgia's voting system
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A federal judge has declined to block the use of Georgia's electronic voting system in a long-running lawsuit alleging that the system is vulnerable to attack and has operational issues that could deprive voters of their constitutional rights. U.S.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Layoffs begin at U.S. health agencies responsible for research, tracking disease and regulating food
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department began receiving notices of dismissal Tuesday in an overhaul ultimately expected to lay off up to 10,000 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat: DDHQ
item.description: The race drew national attention and was one of the first elections to gauge voters' feelings about Trump's first few months in office.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court appears poised to revive terror victims suit against Palestinian groups
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday seemed likely to uphold a law allowing Americans injured by acts of terror in the Middle East to take Palestinian leadership groups to U.S. courts for damages.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court seems ready to let religious groups opt out of unemployment compensation laws
item.description: The case was brought by a chapter of Catholic Charities in Wisconsin, which says that it should be able to opt out of the mandatory state unemployment compensation system.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: White House says it’s ’case closed’ on the Signal group chat review
item.description: Last week, the White House said the National Security Council, the White House counsel office and President Trump adviser Elon Musk were all looking into the mishap.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Senate Democrat will block Trump’s VA nominees to protest cuts
item.description: Sen. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) says he will put a hold on President Trump’s nominees to lead the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to protest cuts at the agency that provides lifelong health care services to military veterans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: House Intel Democrats call for damage assessment on Signal chat
item.description: Democrats on the House Intelligence Committee on Monday requested an independent assessment into Trump administration officials' use of Signal to discuss an airstrike, asking that Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard initiate such a process and delegate it to another official.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Inside a Coast Guard border crackdown
item.description: Chief Peter Nelson, officer in charge for Coast Guard Station San Diego, described escalating confrontations with smugglers.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he expects Musk to eventually return to running companies
item.description: President Trump on Monday said he expects Elon Musk will eventually return to running his companies after his work overhauling the federal government. A reporter noted that Musk, the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, is a special government employee, which means he can only work for 130 days in his role.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota insurance costs could continue to rise under new Trump tariffs
item.description: Tariffs are taxes on goods imported from other countries, but these new taxes are not just impacting the prices of our T-shirts and televisions. Tariffs may even lead to higher insurance rates here at home.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Gretchen Brown, MPR News

item.title: Tennessee bill to requires schools to teach ‘success sequence’ of life path
item.description: The "Success Sequence Act" would require schools to teach the positive outcomes associated with completing the following in the following order: graduate high school, enter the workforce or go to college, get married, and then have children.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 6 things borrowers should know about federal student loans right now
item.description: NPR has spent the past few weeks catching up with student loan experts and asking the Trump administration for clarity on some of borrowers’ biggest questions.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC
